page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-512GB is a memory-optimized server instance utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 64 physical cores and 64 vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture, powered by AMD EPYC 7542 processors running at 2.0 GHz. The system features 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is positioned in the bottom 10 percent, and single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show average results. However, multi-threaded capabilities are more robust, with multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, PassMark CPU scores, and PassMark physics tests all ranking in the top 25 percent. Memory performance is also notable, with small-block cached read bandwidth and large-block write bandwidth both scoring in the top 25 percent.

The instance demonstrates strong capabilities in specific database and inference tasks, achieving 67.51 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and strong prompt processing speeds for small language models. Conversely, text generation for medium-sized language models is weak, falling into the bottom 25 percent. Given the high memory capacity and shared CPU allocation, this server is configured for memory-intensive workloads, multi-threaded data processing, and caching layers rather than raw single-threaded compute tasks.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-512GB is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM. Powered by AMD EPYC 7542 processors running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this KVM-based virtual machine offers 8.0 GB of memory per core but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal a significant contrast: while single-core performance is poor, multi-threaded capabilities are strong, as evidenced by top-quartile scores in PassMark CPU tests and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with small-block cached reads and large-block writes ranking in the top 25 percent. This resource profile makes the instance suitable for memory-intensive applications, Redis caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing workloads, though it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or heavy text generation on medium-sized language models.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-512GB is a memory-optimized KVM server with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 512 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-threaded performance and high memory bandwidth.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-512GB is a memory-optimized KVM server featuring 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and strong multi-threaded performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring sixty-four AMD EPYC cores and half a terabyte of RAM for multi-threaded workloads.

512.0 GB of RAM providing 8.0 GB of memory per core
Strong multi-threaded performance and high memory write bandwidth
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for Redis caching, database operations, and multi-threaded data processing
